days.title: Arrival & Gion District
days.morning: Check in and walk through **Gion**, Kyoto's most famous geisha district. The wooden machiya townhouses along Hanamikoji Street are perfectly preserved.
days.afternoon: Visit **Yasaka Shrine** at the end of Shijo Street. Free entry, open 24h. Walk up through **Maruyama Park** to decompress after the journey.
days.evening: Dinner in Gion — try a traditional **kaiseki** set menu or ramen at a local spot on Nishiki Market's side streets.
days.gem: Arrive at Gion just after 6pm on weekdays — you may spot a real geiko or maiko walking to their engagements.

days.title: Fushimi Inari & Nishiki Market
days.morning: **Fushimi Inari Shrine** — arrive before 8am for an empty lower trail. Walk past the first third to leave the crowds behind. The upper forest is extraordinary.
days.afternoon: **Nishiki Market** — Kyoto's kitchen. Try tamagoyaki, fresh tofu, pickled vegetables, and skewered octopus. Never crowded before noon.
days.evening: Pontocho alley for dinner — a narrow lantern-lit lane packed with restaurants overlooking the Kamo River.
days.gem: The very top of Fushimi Inari (233m) takes 2h round trip and is almost deserted — the view over Osaka on clear days is stunning.

days.title: Arashiyama & Bamboo Grove
days.morning: **Arashiyama Bamboo Grove** — go at 7am before tour buses arrive. Walk through to **Okochi Sanso Villa** garden (¥1000, includes tea).
days.afternoon: **Tenryu-ji Temple** — UNESCO World Heritage garden with a stunning borrowed landscape of the Arashiyama mountains. Then walk along the river.
days.evening: Return to central Kyoto. Light dinner at a tofu restaurant in Arashiyama or back in Gion.
days.gem: Rent a rowboat on the Oi River for ¥1500/hour — the view of the mountains from the water is completely different from the shore.

days.title: Nijo Castle & Nishijin
days.morning: **Nijo Castle** — the shogun's Kyoto residence. The nightingale floors (designed to squeak as an alarm system) are fascinating. Gardens are excellent in any season.
days.afternoon: **Nishijin textile district** — the historic centre of Kyoto's silk weaving industry. Visit Nishijin Textile Center for a free kimono show at 2pm.
days.evening: Drinks at a standing bar in Sanjo or Kawaramachi. Kyoto's craft beer scene is small but excellent.
days.gem: The Nijo Castle cherry trees are the most photographed in Japan — but the inner garden pond in autumn is equally spectacular and half as busy.

days.title: Philosopher's Path & Silver Pavilion
days.morning: Walk the **Philosopher's Path** — a 2km canal-side trail lined with cherry trees connecting the major temple districts. Best between 8-10am.
days.afternoon: **Ginkaku-ji (Silver Pavilion)** at the north end of the path. Despite the name, it was never actually covered in silver — the story is part of its charm. Sand garden is exceptional.
days.evening: Dinner in the Okazaki area — several excellent restaurants near Heian Shrine.
days.gem: The viewpoint hill behind Ginkaku-ji gives a panorama over the entire path and eastern Kyoto — most visitors miss the 5-minute climb.

days.title: Kinkaku-ji & Ryoan-ji
days.morning: **Kinkaku-ji (Golden Pavilion)** — arrive at opening time (9am) before the crowds. The reflection in the mirror pond is best on still mornings.
days.afternoon: **Ryoan-ji Temple** — home of Japan's most famous rock garden. 15 stones arranged so you can never see all 15 at once from any angle. Spend time sitting with it.
days.evening: Farewell dinner — splurge on a full kaiseki experience or try Kyoto-style sushi (different from Tokyo — milder, more delicate).
days.gem: The large pond behind Ryoan-ji (Kyoyochi Pond) predates the rock garden by 400 years and is completely uncrowded — walk the full circuit.

days.title: Fushimi Sake District & Departure
days.morning: **Fushimi sake district** — Kyoto's brewing neighbourhood. Visit Gekkeikan Okura Sake Museum (¥600). Walk the canal paths lined with sake warehouses.
days.afternoon: Last shopping at **Teramachi covered arcade** — antiques, ceramics, tea, Japanese knives. Head to Kyoto Station for shinkansen.
days.evening: Departure from Kyoto Station. The station building itself is an architectural landmark — worth 30 minutes to explore.
days.gem: Toba Fushimi district has a 1.5km sake warehouse canal walk that almost no tourists do — completely authentic neighbourhood feeling.
